title: Aureline framework-certainty row record
description: Boundary schema for the framework_certainty_row_record frozen in docs/framework/framework_certainty_and_source_sync_contract.md. The row is the cross-surface projection every route explorer, component-tree surface, preview card, notebook framework-context inspector, diagnostics lane, AI-assist context lane, and support export reads when it needs one stable answer to the question 'what framework knowledge does Aureline have for this subject, and where did that knowledge come from?'. The row carries one of six subject kinds (detected_framework, route_mapping, component_tree_node, runtime_target, source_synchronization, fallback_inference_reason), one of six framework-certainty classes re-exported verbatim from schemas/language/result_provenance.schema.json (framework_not_applicable, framework_proven, framework_inferred, framework_imported, framework_stale, framework_unavailable), one of eight primary-source classes re-exported verbatim (framework_pack_analysis, framework_pack_artifact, project_graph_projection, language_server_signal, build_adapter_signal, imported_snapshot, ai_inference, none), one of fourteen fallback-inference reasons explaining downgrades, the closed framework-adapter family from schemas/preview/preview_snapshot.schema.json, the closed certainty-source vocabulary from schemas/language/result_provenance.schema.json, optional notebook-binding and runtime-target blocks, and optional route-mapping and component-tree blocks for the route_mapping_subject and component_tree_node_subject rows. The schema enforces the rule that framework_proven requires a framework-pack primary source, a non-empty proving-artifact list, and a non-empty source-of-certainty chain; that language-server-only, build-adapter-only, project-graph-only, imported-snapshot, and AI-inference primary sources may NEVER produce a framework_proven row; that framework_imported requires imported_snapshot; that framework_unavailable requires the none primary source and an empty proving-artifact list; that route_mapping_subject requires a route-mapping block; that component_tree_node_subject requires a component-tree block; that source_synchronization_subject requires a source-sync chip ref; that the notebook framework-context surface requires a notebook-binding block; and that fallback_inference_reason_subject rows MUST cite a non-trivial fallback reason. Raw source text, raw notebook payloads, raw provider logs, raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw hostnames, raw IP addresses, and raw secret material never cross this boundary; the row carries opaque ids, typed vocabulary, and export-safe summaries only. Adding a new surface_class, subject_kind_class, framework_certainty_class, framework_primary_source_class, framework_adapter_class, fallback_inference_reason_class, route_mapping_class, component_tree_node_class, certainty_source_class, generated_note_class, or notebook_note_class value is additive-minor and bumps framework_certainty_row_schema_version; repurposing an existing field is breaking and requires a new decision row.

framework_certainty_row_schema_version.description: Integer schema version. Current value is 1. Bumped on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields and additive enum values do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log, export, or persist. Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw IP addresses, raw hostnames, raw bearer tokens, and raw secret values never appear here.

redacted_label.description: Reviewer-facing short label safe to render in framework chrome. Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw account handles, and raw secret values never appear here.

short_summary.description: Reviewer-facing sentence safe to render in framework chrome and export review.

surface_class.description: Closed surface vocabulary every framework-certainty row may project to. Mirrors the route-explorer, component-tree, preview-card, notebook-framework-context, diagnostics, AI-assist, and support-export consumers. Surfaces project the same row instead of inventing private framework-knowledge fields.

subject_kind_class.description: Closed subject-kind vocabulary. detected_framework_subject covers 'what framework do we believe this code is using?'. route_mapping_subject covers a single URL/route → handler/page mapping. component_tree_node_subject covers one component-tree node (or root). runtime_target_subject covers what runtime/preview target the framework is bound to. source_synchronization_subject covers source-vs-runtime alignment. fallback_inference_reason_subject is a row that exists only to disclose why a downgrade happened (no proven knowledge claimed).

framework_certainty_class.description: Re-export of framework_certainty_class from schemas/language/result_provenance.schema.json. Mirrors the result-provenance and capability-negotiation vocabulary so framework rows, navigation results, diagnostics, notebook context packets, and AI-assist explanations share one certainty language.

framework_primary_source_class.description: Re-export of framework_primary_source_class from schemas/language/result_provenance.schema.json. Only framework_pack_analysis and framework_pack_artifact may yield framework_proven.

framework_adapter_class.description: Re-export of framework_adapter_class from schemas/preview/preview_snapshot.schema.json. unsupported_or_unclaimed_adapter MUST NOT carry framework_proven.

freshness_class.description: Re-export of freshness_class from schemas/language/result_provenance.schema.json. Shared freshness ladder.

certainty_source_class.description: Re-export of certainty_source_class from schemas/language/result_provenance.schema.json. Each entry in the source-of-certainty chain cites one of these source classes.

certainty_source_row.description: One entry in the source-of-certainty chain. Mirrors the certainty_source_row from schemas/language/result_provenance.schema.json; this contract does not mint a parallel chain dialect.

fallback_inference_reason_class.description: Closed fallback-inference reason vocabulary. no_fallback_proven_directly is reserved for framework_proven rows where no downgrade applies. The remaining values name the concrete reason a row landed below framework_proven so framework-aware surfaces can disclose it without per-surface free text.

notebook_binding_block.description: Notebook binding for rows projected to the notebook framework-context surface.

route_mapping_class.description: Closed route-mapping outcome vocabulary. route_mapped_to_handler_proven is admissible only when the row is framework_proven. route_handler_unknown / route_pattern_unknown_to_runtime / route_blocked_no_runtime forbid framework_proven.

route_pattern_ref.description: Opaque ref to the framework-pack-typed route pattern (route id / pattern id). Raw URLs, raw query strings, and raw path segments never appear here.

route_handler_ref.description: Opaque ref to the resolved handler/page for the route. Null when the row's route_mapping_class indicates an unknown or blocked handler.

component_tree_node_class.description: Closed component-tree-node vocabulary. component_node_proven is admissible only when the row is framework_proven. component_node_imported is admissible only when the row is framework_imported. component_node_stale is admissible only when the row is framework_stale. component_node_unmappable is admissible only when the row is framework_inferred or framework_unavailable.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional fixture metadata block carried only by worked fixtures; not part of the production payload.

subject_ref.description: Optional opaque ref naming the concrete subject (a route id, a component node id, a runtime id). Raw absolute paths, raw URLs, and raw symbol bodies never appear here.

framework_pack_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the framework pack that supplied the evidence. Null for framework_not_applicable / framework_unavailable rows.

framework_adapter_revision_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the framework-adapter revision pin. Null when the row carries no adapter binding.

proving_artifact_refs.description: Opaque refs to the framework-pack proving artifacts that justify the certainty class. framework_proven requires minItems 1; framework_unavailable / framework_not_applicable require maxItems 0.

source_of_certainty_chain.description: Source-of-certainty chain. framework_proven requires minItems 1 with at least one framework_pack_analysis or framework_pack_artifact entry.

runtime_target_block.description: Optional runtime-target block. Required (non-null) for runtime_target_subject rows.

notebook_binding_block.description: Optional notebook-binding block. Required (non-null) for surface_class = notebook_framework_context_surface.

route_mapping_block.description: Optional route-mapping block. Required (non-null) for subject_kind_class = route_mapping_subject; forbidden (null) for any other subject.

component_tree_block.description: Optional component-tree block. Required (non-null) for subject_kind_class = component_tree_node_subject; forbidden (null) for any other subject.

source_sync_chip_record_ref.description: Optional ref to the source_sync_chip_record this row composes with. Required (non-null) for subject_kind_class = source_synchronization_subject.

allOf.description: framework_proven requires a framework-pack primary source, a non-empty proving-artifact list, a non-empty source-of-certainty chain, no_fallback_proven_directly, freshness_class != stale AND != unverified, and framework_adapter_class != unsupported_or_unclaimed_adapter.
framework_primary_source_class in {language_server_signal, build_adapter_signal, project_graph_projection, imported_snapshot, ai_inference, none} forbids framework_proven.
framework_imported requires framework_primary_source_class = imported_snapshot.
framework_stale requires freshness_class in {stale, unverified}.
framework_unavailable requires framework_primary_source_class = none and an empty proving-artifact list.
framework_not_applicable requires framework_primary_source_class = none and an empty proving-artifact list.
framework_adapter_class = unsupported_or_unclaimed_adapter forbids framework_proven.
fallback_inference_reason_class = no_fallback_proven_directly is reserved for framework_proven rows.

fallback_inference_reason_class in {language_server_only_evidence, build_adapter_only_evidence, project_graph_only_evidence, imported_snapshot_only_evidence, ai_inference_only_evidence, generic_language_signal_only} forbids framework_proven.
subject_kind_class = route_mapping_subject requires a non-null route_mapping_block.
subject_kind_class != route_mapping_subject requires route_mapping_block = null.
subject_kind_class = component_tree_node_subject requires a non-null component_tree_block.
subject_kind_class != component_tree_node_subject requires component_tree_block = null.
subject_kind_class = runtime_target_subject requires a non-null runtime_target_block.
subject_kind_class = source_synchronization_subject requires a non-null source_sync_chip_record_ref.
subject_kind_class = fallback_inference_reason_subject forbids fallback_inference_reason_class = no_fallback_proven_directly and forbids framework_proven.
surface_class = notebook_framework_context_surface requires a non-null notebook_binding_block.
route_mapping_block.route_mapping_class = route_mapped_to_handler_proven requires framework_certainty_class = framework_proven.

route_mapping_block.route_mapping_class in {route_handler_unknown, route_pattern_unknown_to_runtime, route_blocked_no_runtime, route_stale_against_current_source, route_mapped_from_imported_evidence} forbids framework_proven.
component_tree_block.component_tree_node_class = component_node_proven requires framework_certainty_class = framework_proven.
component_tree_block.component_tree_node_class = component_node_imported requires framework_certainty_class = framework_imported.
component_tree_block.component_tree_node_class = component_node_stale requires framework_certainty_class = framework_stale.
component_tree_block.component_tree_node_class = component_node_unmappable requires framework_certainty_class in {framework_inferred, framework_unavailable}.

framework_certainty_row_record.description: One framework-certainty row record emitted by a route explorer, component-tree, preview card, notebook framework-context, diagnostics, AI-assist, or support-export surface.
